title: SpaceX and Amazon spar over satellite deployments
summary: SpaceX and Amazon are in a dispute over Amazon's satellite deployment plans, with SpaceX alleging safety risks. Amazon denies the claims and plans to revise its approach.

title: Europe’s strategic autonomy in space will define its role in the ‘second space age’
summary: Europe's future in space hinges on achieving strategic autonomy to avoid reliance on foreign technology in the 'second space age'. This autonomy is deemed essential for Europe to access, control, and utilize space effectively.

title: China’s commercial Tianlong-3 rocket fails on debut launch
summary: China's commercial Tianlong-3 rocket failed during its debut launch due to an anomaly during ascent. The failure marks a setback for Space Pioneer's ambitions in the commercial space sector.

title: Artemis 2 heads to the moon
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission has begun its journey to the moon following a successful maneuver on April 2. This marks a significant step toward the agency's lunar exploration goals.

title: Swift spacecraft reorientation buys time for reboost mission
summary: NASA adjusted the operations of the Swift spacecraft in a decaying orbit to extend its operational time before a planned reboost mission later this year. This reorientation helps preserve the spacecraft's functionality while preparing for an orbit-raising maneuver.

title: Former Sierra Space CEO Tom Vice to lead Astrion
summary: Tom Vice, former CEO of Sierra Space, has been appointed to lead Astrion, a Huntsville-based defense contractor specializing in systems engineering and space mission assurance.

title: Optical terminals still a bottleneck in Pentagon’s proliferated constellation
summary: The Pentagon faces challenges with optical terminals for its proliferated constellation, as current capabilities are insufficient to meet demand. GP Sandhoo highlights the gap in meeting the required number of optical communications terminals.

title: Pentagon awards Raytheon $45 million for GPS ground system as program future is reassessed
summary: The Pentagon awarded Raytheon a $45 million unpriced change order to support satellite launches while officials reassess the future of a long-delayed GPS ground system program.

title: Moog Technology Successfully Steers Artemis II Launch
summary: Moog Inc. provided critical technology to successfully steer the Artemis II launch. The company specializes in precision motion and fluid control systems.

title: Carmel Ortiz on the evolving landscape of satellite communications
summary: Carmel Ortiz, SES's senior vice president of medium-Earth-orbit constellation programs, discusses new satellite communication technologies on Space Minds. The interview highlights advancements in the evolving satellite communications landscape.

title: NASA is sending astronauts back to the moon. Can you see the Artemis 4 landing sites from Earth?
summary: NASA's Artemis Program aims to return astronauts to the moon, focusing on the lunar south pole. The mission raises questions about the visibility of potential landing sites from Earth.

title: How to watch the new 'lost' Doctor Who episodes from anywhere in the world
summary: Two previously lost Doctor Who episodes, 'The Nightmare Begins' and 'Devil's Planet,' have been recovered and are now available to watch globally. Fans can stream these episodes from anywhere in the world.

title: Pulsars to the extreme: Spinning dead stars found blasting radio signals from the 'edge of their magnetic reach'
summary: Astronomers discovered that rapidly spinning pulsars emit radio signals from the edges of their magnetic fields. This finding reveals new insights into the extreme behavior of these dead stars.

title: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts are cruising to the moon. So why are they doing CPR tests today?
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts are preparing for their moon mission, including conducting CPR tests. Flight Day 3 of their mission may proceed at a more relaxed pace.

title: DJI Avata 360 drone review
summary: DJI has released the Avata 360, its first drone capable of 360-degree flight, offering both camera and FPV (first-person view) drone capabilities with a motion controller and compatible goggles.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts head for the moon after make-or-break engine burn (video)
summary: Artemis 2 astronauts successfully completed a critical engine burn, propelling the Orion capsule from Earth orbit toward the moon. This milestone marks a key step in NASA's preparations for the upcoming lunar mission.

title: Lucky airplane passengers capture NASA's Artemis 2 moon launch from the sky
summary: Airplane passengers captured NASA's Artemis 2 moon rocket launch from the sky, sharing videos of the historic event.

title: Watch comet C/2026 A1 plunge toward the sun online this week
summary: Comet C/2026 A1 (MAPS), a rare sungrazing comet, will be visible online as it approaches the sun this week. This event offers a unique opportunity for astronomers and the public to observe its trajectory.

title: 'The universe shall quake in my shadow' — 'Masters of the Universe' looks like the summer's most metal movie
summary: A trailer for the upcoming movie 'Masters of the Universe' (MOTU) featuring Jared Leto as Skeletor has been released, showcasing a dramatic confrontation with He-Man. The film is being marketed as a high-energy, action-packed summer release.

title: Hello, World
summary: NASA astronaut Reid Wiseman captured a stunning image of Earth from the Orion spacecraft during the Artemis II mission, showcasing auroras and zodiacal light. This marks the first images of Earth taken from the spacecraft following the translunar injection burn.

title: Barents Sea Tied to Low Arctic Sea Ice
summary: Patches of open water in the Barents Sea contributed to the lowest Arctic sea ice extent in March 2026, tying with the lowest maximum observed in the satellite record.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Mission Leaves Earth Orbit for Flight around Moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission has successfully completed a translunar injection burn, marking the first time astronauts will fly around the Moon in over 50 years. The six-minute engine firing propelled the Orion spacecraft out of Earth's orbit toward the Moon.

title: Artemis II Astronauts Launch to Moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission will launch four astronauts on a 10-day journey around the Moon and back in April 2026. The mission includes NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen.

title: Réunion Island Lava Reaches the Sea
summary: The February 2026 eruption at Piton de la Fournaise on Réunion Island has lasted longer and produced more lava than recent eruptions. The lava has now reached the sea.

title: Liftoff! NASA Launches Astronauts on Historic Artemis Moon Mission
summary: NASA successfully launched the Artemis II mission, marking the first crewed lunar flyby in over 50 years. The SLS rocket lifted off from Kennedy Space Center, carrying four astronauts aboard the Orion spacecraft.

title: March of the Harmattan
summary: Strong Harmattan winds in March 2026 carried Saharan dust across northwestern Africa and the Canary Islands, reducing visibility and triggering alerts.

title: Godspeed, Artemis II!
summary: NASA astronaut Jessica Meir shared a photo of the Artemis program patch aboard the International Space Station, highlighting preparations for the upcoming Artemis II mission to return humans to the Moon.

title: Landsat Reveals Reservoir Changes and Bathymetry
summary: Researchers used Landsat data to analyze reservoir changes and bathymetry in two recent studies. This highlights the satellite's role in advancing water resource management and geological understanding.

title: Fires Tear Through Nebraska Grasslands
summary: Extreme fire activity tore through Nebraska grasslands in March 2026 due to dry, warm, and windy conditions across the U.S. Great Plains.

title: Artemis II Orion performs TLI Burn — Now headed to the Moon
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft successfully performed the Trans-Lunar Injection (TLI) burn, marking a historic milestone 50 years after Apollo as it heads toward the Moon. This achievement is a critical step for the Artemis II mission, paving the way for future lunar exploration.

title: Artemis II returns humanity to the Moon with launch on Wednesday evening
summary: The Artemis II mission aims to return humanity to the Moon with a launch scheduled for Wednesday evening. This marks a significant milestone in space exploration following the Apollo 17 mission in 1972.

title: Launch Preview: SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz launches comprise busy launch manifest
summary: Nine launches are scheduled this week, featuring rockets like SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz from locations including Florida, California, and Russia.

title: America’s Rocket Factory comes Full Circle with Artemis
summary: Rocket Factory is involved in NASA's Artemis program, marking a significant milestone in America's return to crewed launches beyond low Earth orbit. The collaboration highlights advancements in space exploration technology.

title: SpaceX delivers 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on Transporter 16
summary: SpaceX launched 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit from California as part of the Transporter 16 mission. This marks another successful rideshare mission for the company.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA announced detailed plans for a Moon Base during its 'Ignition' event, shifting focus away from the Lunar Gateway. This marks a significant pivot in the agency's lunar exploration strategy.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider based in Germany, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, planned for launch in 2028. Administrator Jared Isaacman and other NASA leaders revealed details about the initiative.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is making significant progress on infrastructure upgrades at Starbase in preparation for the next-generation Block 3 Starships, aiming to increase launch cadence. Engineers are actively working on ground systems to support the upcoming vehicle upgrades.

title: Launch Preview: Russia to debut Soyuz-5; Falcon 9 and Atlas V to launch internet satellites
summary: Russia is set to debut its new Soyuz-5 rocket, while the U.S. will launch internet satellites using Falcon 9 and Atlas V. Multiple countries, including China, New Zealand, and Norway, are also preparing for upcoming launches.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on space industry news, but no specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past 50 years. This publication celebrates ESA's milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A silicon chip has been developed that integrates multiple functionalities, potentially revolutionizing space electronics. This advancement could enhance satellite and spacecraft systems by improving efficiency and reducing complexity.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly caused a loss of communication.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting a series of events to discuss the implementation of decisions from the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25). These events aim to inform industry about upcoming activities, priorities, and opportunities within ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in ESA's Large Space Simulator. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is expected to launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical heart. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, mandating its use by all suppliers for financial proposals in ESA Tender Actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a 3D-printed, football-sized lens antenna to improve satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a simpler and cheaper alternative to bulky existing solutions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly on Proba-3's Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Insurance is Commercial Space Nuclear’s Biggest Headache
summary: Insurance is identified as the biggest challenge for commercial space nuclear missions, with experts calling it a 'show stopper'. The issue was highlighted during an industry seminar.

title: Charts Defining the Space Industry in Q1 2026
summary: The article discusses key trends and data points defining the space industry in Q1 2026, highlighting a resurgence in Earth Observation (EO) activities. It emphasizes the growing importance of EO in the commercial space sector.

title: CLPS Companies Excited For NASA’s ‘Opportunity Bomb’ Lunar Plan
summary: NASA announced a plan for a lunar base supplied via monthly uncrewed lunar landings starting next year, which has CLPS companies eager to participate. CLPS (Commercial Lunar Payload Services) providers are highly motivated by this 'opportunity bomb' lunar initiative.

title: Phantom Space Acquires Thermal Management Technologies
summary: Phantom Space has acquired Thermal Management Technologies, a company specializing in advanced satellite thermal components for in-space applications.

title: Artemis II Is On The Way To The Moon
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is en route to the Moon with a crew including the first woman and first person of color to head to the Moon, as well as the first non-American astronaut. The mission marks a historic step in space exploration.

title: Exclusive: In-Orbit Manufacturing Startup Dispatch Emerges from Stealth
summary: Dispatch, an in-orbit manufacturing startup, has emerged from stealth mode with $500,000 in funding. The company plans to build an uncrewed space station to host manufacturing infrastructure.

title: Antaris Raises $28M Series A
summary: Antaris has raised $28 million in a Series A funding round led by WestWave Capital, with participation from Lockheed Martin Ventures and other new investors.

title: Top Space Lawmaker on Moon Base, Artemis Plans
summary: A top space lawmaker expressed strong support for establishing a Moon base as a strategic step towards future Mars missions. The comments highlight growing enthusiasm for NASA's Artemis program and long-term lunar exploration goals.

title: Voyager and Icarus Team Up on ISS Robotics Demo
summary: Voyager and Icarus are collaborating on a robotics demonstration aboard the International Space Station (ISS), blending real-world space operations with a futuristic narrative. This partnership highlights advancements in ISS robotics technology.

title: Q&A With Lunar Base Manager Carlos Garcia-Galan
summary: Carlos Garcia-Galan transitioned from NASA’s lunar-orbiting habitat program to overseeing the agency’s plan for a rapid lunar surface base. He discussed this shift during NASA HQ’s Ignition event.

title: Astroscale and Exotrail to Co-Develop Servicing Mission
summary: Astroscale and Exotrail will collaborate on a satellite de-orbiting mission, combining Japanese and French expertise in on-orbit servicing to safely remove satellites from orbit.

title: In-Orbit Logistics Companies Call for Clearer Demand Signals from Gov
summary: In-orbit logistics companies are urging the government to provide clearer demand signals. The Space Development Agency acknowledges the challenges of operating in space.

title: Canada’s Northwestel to Provide Broadband With Telesat Lightspeed Capacity
summary: Canada’s Northwestel has signed a multi-year contract with Telesat to use capacity from its Lightspeed satellite constellation to provide broadband services. Northwestel operates in Canada’s northern regions, including Nunavut and the Northwest Territories.

title: Phantom Space Acquires Component Supplier Thermal Management Technologies
summary: Phantom Space Corporation acquired Thermal Management Technologies (TMT) to support its orbital data center constellation development and vertical integration strategy. The acquisition aims to accelerate the company's planned space-based data center projects.

title: ArkEdge Space to Work With ABIT For IoT Service Rollout
summary: ArkEdge Space and ABIT Corporation signed an MoU to jointly develop IoT solutions and satellite communication devices. The partnership aims to advance satellite-based IoT services.

title: NASA Launches Artemis II in Further Step Toward a Moon Return
summary: NASA launched Artemis II, its first crewed mission under the Artemis program, to test the Orion spacecraft’s life support systems with astronauts aboard. This mission marks a significant step toward returning humans to the Moon.

title: CanarySat CEO Antonio Abad Outlines the Sovereign, Secure Approach Behind the Magec Constellation
summary: CanarySat, backed by Arquimea, unveiled its Magec constellation, a secure Ka-band LEO satellite network, during SATShow Week. CEO Antonio Abad emphasized the constellation's sovereign and secure approach.

title: Bloomberg, CNBC Report SpaceX Submitted Confidential Filing for IPO
summary: SpaceX has submitted a confidential filing for its initial public offering (IPO) with the SEC, as reported by Bloomberg and CNBC. A confidential filing allows the company to draft its IPO registration privately before going public.

title: Measat Appoints New CTO
summary: Measat Global appointed John Loke as its new Chief Technology Officer to lead the company's digital transformation and regional strategy.

title: Live in the Booth: Arianespace CEO David Cavaillolès Previews Ariane 6 Ramp-Up
summary: Arianespace CEO David Cavaillolès discusses the ramp-up of the Ariane 6 rocket ahead of its next mission in April. The company is currently executing a launch campaign for Amazon's Leo satellites after its first major customer launch in February.
